title: Uueg Tching
content:

{{Quote|There are three ways to defeat your enemy. The first, and most obvious, is to better him in a trial of force. The best way is to have him destroy himself; few enemies are so obliging. The middle way is to destroy your enemy from within. Judicious application of the middle way shall make your blows more effective if you later take the way of force. From the middle way it is also possible to push your enemy onto the path of self-destruction.|Uueg Tching's oft-quoted saying|<ref name="imp" />}}
'''Uueg Tching''', a [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of the [[Kitel Phard Dynasty]] and the fifty-fourth [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] of [[Atrisia/Legends|Atrisia]], was famous for his ability to keep order through espionage, [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]], and deception. Through his direction, he was able to subvert over thirty rebellions to his rule; Uueg's empire was used as a basis for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], which was founded by [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] some 3,000 years after Uueg's [[Death/Legends|death]].

Uueg's ascension to Emperor coincided with the discovery of several additional continents on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], but he was able to unify the continents through oppression, until the [[Atrisian Empire]] ruled the entire [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. Uueg was revered by many of his subjects, and he was constantly trailed by a cadre of scribes, who documented his teachings on scrolls, which eventually became known as the ''[[Sayings]]'', lessons he hoped would help his descendants thrive when they reached power themselves. After his death, few members of the [[Kitel Phard Dynasty|Tching Dynasty]] grasped his teachings, though those who did had very successful reigns. The ''Sayings'' became famous across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] centuries after Uueg's time; they were quoted at [[Commission for the Protection of the Republic/Legends|Commission for the Protection of the Republic]] rallies, in historical documents, and were taught to all new recruits of [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]].

==Biography==
===Rule===
{{Quote|Look at the Atrisi system, for example. The principal planet still recalls the Kitel Phard Dynasty with pride. These rulers unified the entire homeworld, eliminating opposition and establishing a golden age. This tale is typical of imperial legends and histories throughout the galaxy, containing those elements that are common to intergalactic memory. Few recall the negative aspects of imperial rule, preferring to remember the power and glory that comes from an overarching code of law and a common tongue.|[[Arhul Hextrophon/Legends|Arhul Hextrophon]]|<ref name="imp" />}}
Uueg Tching was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="atlas" /> born over 3,000 years before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], at the height of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. A native of [[Atrisia/Legends|Atrisia]] (also known as Kitel Phard), prime [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of the [[Atrisi system]], and a member of the [[Kitel Phard Dynasty]], he eventually became the fifty-fourth Atrisian [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]].<ref name="imp" /> The Kitel Phard Dynasty was almost as old as the Republic itself and was known for its violent philosophy—all disputes were settled with considerable [[Blood/Legends|bloodshed]] and open warfare. Uueg sought to change this.<ref name="aj" />

Uueg had a keen mind for espionage, deception, diplomacy, and strategy, and he integrated each into his modus operandi, using them to great effect during his reign.<ref name="aj" /> Shortly after his ascension, several new continents were discovered on Atrisia, and Uueg was tasked with unifying the world and annexing the recently-discovered continents.<ref name="imp" /> Using various means—such as infiltration, [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]], and espionage—that were far more subtle than what was considered normal on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], Uueg was able to successfully increase the [[Atrisian Empire]]'s rule, until he controlled the entire planet, both directly and indirectly. Under Uueg, a previously-unknown order was kept,<ref name="aj" /> and over thirty rebellions against him were quashed. His rule was considered by many to be tyrannical, though the majority of his subjects respected him, believing that he and the Kitel Phard Dynasty had established a golden age.<ref name="imp" /> Uueg also instituted [[Atrisian script]] as the native alphabet, expanding its usage to a planetwide scale for the first time since it was standardized by the [[First Atrisian Emperor]].<ref name="tww">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/writtenword/index.html?page=1|text=The Written Word|int=The Written Word}}</ref>

Uueg was revered by many on his native planet, and during his time as Emperor, a group of scribes followed him everywhere, recording his teachings on how to rule an empire. He began to teach several potential heirs with cold-blooded parables and analogies.<ref name="aj" /> One of his parables talked of a gardener, who was attentive enough to notice signs of decay within a [[Tree/Legends|tree]] he was caring for, and to cut through healthy bark to heal the tree. According to Uueg, those who utilized the tree were in the gardener's debt, for he had maintained the tree. The tree represented an empire, such as Uueg's; the rotting corruption of any kind; the person using its shade, the subjects of the empire; and the gardener the being who presided over the empire and kept order.<ref name="rep">{{InsiderCite|70|Republic HoloNet News Core Edition 14:9:04}}</ref> In another of his teachings, he stated there were three ways to defeat an enemy: via direct confrontation, to have him destroy himself, or to destroy him from within. Uueg advocated using all three methods when dealing with enemies.<ref name="imp" />

He taught these lessons—which were later recorded by his scribes and dubbed the ''[[Sayings]]''—to his heirs, and Uueg intended them to be a guide to his descendants. The scribes' writing was written in black ink on several scrolls, in [[Atrisian script]], but many of Uueg's handwritten addenda were etched in red ink. Eventually, Uueg perished, and was replaced by a new Atrisian Empire of the [[Kitel Phard Dynasty|Tching Dynasty]].<ref name="aj" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|An enlightened despot, he is remembered by Atrisians for his skill in diplomacy, in war, and in maintaining order.|Corellia Antilles|<ref name="aj" />}}
Few of Uueg's descendants really mastered his teachings, though those that did—[[Oeana Tching]], the so-called "Invisible Empress," and [[Eoaq the Expansive|Eoaq]] the Expansive were the most well-known—were extremely successful in their reigns. The original document of the ''Sayings'' was kept safely in the [[Rare Books Department]] of the [[Atrisian Imperial Historical Library]] on Atrisia for over 3,000 years. Around [[1500 BBY]], a small number of copies of the ''Sayings'' was printed, in a propaganda effort by the then-Emperor of Atrisia, who wanted to showcase the supposed superiority of his ancestor in an attempt to make himself seem stronger to his fellow nobles.<ref name="aj" />

[[File:Uueg_Tching_Sayings_SWAJ14.png|thumb|right|250px|The original manuscript of Uueg Tching's ''Sayings''.]]
Uueg's empire,<ref name="aj" /> like the majority of such systems,<ref name="imp" /> only lasted for so long, and shortly prior to [[539 BBY]], the [[Atrisian Parliament]] wrested power from the nobles; one of the Parliament's first acts, in an attempt to highlight the power they had assumed and to raise money, was to publicly release the ''Sayings'' on an easily-accessible database.<ref name="aj" /> Hundreds of years later, the ''Sayings'', particularly Uueg's quote about how best to deal with enemies, were widely-known across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="rep" /><ref name="imp" /> [[Tra-Skan-Lor]], the [[Director/Legends|Director]] of the [[Special Acquisitions Branch]] of the [[Library of the Republic]] was intrigued by the wisdom of Tching's ''Sayings'' and made his subordinates study them, causing the work to become deeply embeded within the Branch's culture.<ref name="The Written Word">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/writtenword/index.html?page=1|text=The Written Word|int=The Written Word}}</ref>

During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], members of the [[Commission for the Protection of the Republic/Legends|Commission for the Protection of the Republic's]] [[SAGroup (COMPOR)/Legends|SAGroup]] publicly quoted Uueg Tching's ''Sayings'' about the routing of corruption during a ceremony on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], meant to show their support for [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]].<ref name="rep" /> Palpatine eventually abolished the Republic and formed the first [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]; it was modeled after the ancient empires of Atrisia, and others.<ref name="imp" /> In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], Palpatine requested that the original manuscript of the ''Sayings'' be transported to the [[Galactic Museum/Legends|Galactic Museum]] on Coruscant, though before long it had again been moved, this time into Palpatine's personal vault of ancient treasures. Historians speculated that [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine simply wanted the scrolls as a trophy, as he had already mastered Uueg's ways.<ref name="aj" /> Uueg's ''Sayings'' were mandatory reading for new recruits of [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]].<ref name="imp" /> The ''Sayings''{{'}} original scrolls were taken to the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] after the [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|Liberation of Coruscant]], and, despite her efforts, [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] historian [[Henrietya Antilles|Corellia Antilles]] was unable to locate any trace of them. She hoped that recovering the scrolls would help improve relations between the New Republic and the [[Atrisian Commonwealth/Legends|Atrisian Commonwealth]].<ref name="aj" /> Uueg's quote on defeating enemies was still well-known as of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], starting in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]].<ref name="fh">''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic I: Remnant]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Even the greatest of trees can rot from within, but the vigilant gardener keeps attentive to the signs of decay. The Gardener is not afraid to cut through the polished skin to tear away a malignant heart, to save the life of that tree. Those who seek shade under that tree would best remember the work of that gardener.|Uueg Tching|<ref name="rep" />}}
Uueg Tching was obsessed with power, and he clung to it with his life. Despite this, he was fiercely patriotic, and cared greatly for his subjects.<ref name="imp" /> He hated corruption, and he did what he could to ensure that it was not present during his regime.<ref name="rep" /> Uueg was a master strategist, and he had always had multiple ways to deal with his enemies. He was merciless during his reign, unifying his planet through violence and defeating those who were unhappy with his reign without remorse.<ref name="imp" /> He cared about the future, and hoped his descendants would utilize his teachings to great effect. Uueg was not above using unscrupulous means to achieve his goals, and espionage, trickery, and cold-blooded assassination were all the tools of his trade.<ref name="aj" />

==Behind the scenes==
The character of Uueg Tching was first mentioned in the first edition of [[Greg Gorden]]'s ''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'', in [[October]] [[1989]].<ref name="imp" />
